title: Google/Gmail Sign-In and Account Joining

requirements.text: Implement Google Sign-In on the Login page so users can authenticate using their Gmail/Google account via the existing Google Identity Services (GSI) script already loaded in index.html. On successful Google sign-in, if the user has no existing profile, open the ProfileSetupModal pre-filled with the Google account's name and email data. If a profile already exists, navigate the user to the home page.

quotes: Continue with Google ko gmail ID joining

acceptanceCriteria: Login page displays a functional 'Continue with Google' button
Clicking the button triggers the Google Identity Services sign-in flow
On successful Google authentication, the parsed JWT user data (name, email, picture) is persisted via the existing useGoogleAuth hook
If no profile exists for the authenticated user, the ProfileSetupModal opens pre-filled with Google account name
If a profile already exists, the user is redirected to the home page
Google sign-in state is reflected in the Layout/navbar (shows user avatar or name from Google auth)

requirements.text: Implement Google Sign-Up on the Signup page so new users can create an account by joining with their Gmail/Google ID. On successful Google authentication for a new user, open the ProfileSetupModal pre-filled with data from the Google JWT (name, profile picture URL) to complete channel setup.

quotes: Continue with Google ko gmail ID joining

acceptanceCriteria: Signup page displays a functional 'Join with Google' button
Clicking the button triggers the Google Identity Services sign-in flow
On successful Google authentication, the useGoogleAuth hook stores the user data
ProfileSetupModal is shown automatically with channel name pre-filled from Google display name
After profile setup is saved via useSaveCallerUserProfile, the user is navigated to the home page
Duplicate sign-in (existing profile) redirects user to home without reopening setup modal

requirements.text: Update the ProfileSetupModal component to accept optional pre-fill props (displayName, avatarUrl) sourced from Google auth data. When these props are provided, pre-populate the channel name input and attempt to display the Google profile picture as the initial avatar preview.

quotes: Continue with Google ko gmail ID joining

acceptanceCriteria: ProfileSetupModal accepts optional googleDisplayName and googleAvatarUrl props
When googleDisplayName is provided, the channel name field is pre-filled with it
When googleAvatarUrl is provided, it is shown as the avatar preview in the modal
User can still override the pre-filled values before submitting
Modal submission saves profile correctly with any user-edited values

requirements.text: Update the Layout/navbar component to display the authenticated Google user's profile picture and display name when signed in via Google (useGoogleAuth hook). Clicking the avatar should navigate to the Profile page. Show a sign-in button when no Google session and no Internet Identity session is active.

quotes: Continue with Google ko gmail ID joining

acceptanceCriteria: When a Google session is active (useGoogleAuth returns a user), the navbar shows the Google profile picture as a circular avatar
Hovering or clicking the avatar shows the user's Google display name
Clicking the avatar navigates to the Profile page
A sign-out option is accessible from the user avatar menu for Google-authenticated users
When neither Google nor Internet Identity session is active, the sign-in button is visible

constraints: Use the existing useGoogleAuth hook and the Google Identity Services script already loaded in index.html — do not introduce a new OAuth library
Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
Internet Identity login must remain fully functional alongside Google sign-in
nonGoals: Backend authentication changes — Google auth is handled client-side via GSI JWT
Facebook, GitHub, or other third-party OAuth providers
Merging Google and Internet Identity accounts into a single identity
